title: edikt artifact sidecar
description: The <artifact>.edikt.yaml sidecar file co-located next to an ADR, invariant, or guideline. Replaces the in-body [edikt:directives:start]/[edikt:directives:end] sentinel block per ADR-027 (sidecar architecture, v0.6.0). Compile reads sidecars verbatim and never mutates the parent .md.
$comment: v1.1 (additive) — paths, scope, prohibitions are optional. KnownFields(true) in the Go decoder makes the v1.1 fields forward-only: a v1.0 reader cannot parse a sidecar that uses them. edikt distributes as a single binary; upgrade as a unit. The schema version stays at 1 because the on-disk shape only widens.

schema_version.description: Sidecar schema version. The directive sidecar uses a discrete integer (1) because its on-disk shape is a v1-or-v2 contract, not a semver-versioned one — bump only when the on-disk shape changes in a way that older readers cannot interpret. PRD/SPEC sidecars (prd-sidecar.v1.schema.json, spec-sidecar.v1.schema.json) deliberately use a string semver ("1.0") for additive evolution within v1.x; the asymmetry is intentional. v0.6.0 ships with version 1.

topic.description: Kebab-case topic identifier used to group directives across artifacts during compile. 1–40 characters, must start with a lowercase letter, may contain lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens.

path.description: Relative path (from the project root) to the parent .md artifact this sidecar describes. Validated by /edikt:doctor — a sidecar whose path doesn't resolve is reported as orphaned.

items.description: Lowercase signal phrase. Used by the routing table in compiled governance to surface this directive set when a task matches the signal.

signals.description: Lowercase, deduplicated keyword/phrase signals that route work to this artifact.

paths.description: v1.1 (optional). File-glob patterns scoping where this artifact's directives apply (e.g. 'tools/edikt/**/*.go', 'templates/hooks/**/*.sh'). Glob syntax is doublestar/v4 compatible. Validated as non-empty strings at parse time; full glob-syntax validation deferred to gov:compile.

scope.description: v1.1 (optional). Lifecycle phases during which this artifact's directives are surfaced. Closed enum: planning | design | implementation | review. ADR Decision blocks default to [design, implementation, review]; INV Statements default to [implementation, review]; ADR architectural prohibitions default to [planning, design, review].

text.description: MUST NOT directive synthesised from a rejected option. Same shape as directives[].text.

derived_from.description: Provenance hint for the prohibition (e.g., 'rejected_option_b'). Optional; auditability only — not used by compile.

prohibitions.description: v1.1 (optional). MUST NOT directives synthesised from rejected '## Considered Options' (ADRs only). Each entry MUST have a verifiable source_excerpt pointing to a Cons: bullet or rejected-option body in the parent .md. Extractor never invents a prohibition that is not literally present in rejected-option text (see Phase 2 Rule C in templates/agents/sidecar-extractor.md).

manual_directives.description: User-authored rules preserved across sidecar regenerations. Always included in the effective rule set. Never overwritten by sidecar-extractor.

suppressed_directives.description: Auto-directives the user rejected. Subtracted from directives[] at gov:compile time. Never overwritten by sidecar-extractor.

reminders.description: Pre-action reminders aggregated into governance.md ## Reminders. Format: 'Before {action} → {check} (ref: ID)'. Extracted from ## Confirmation (ADRs) and ## Enforcement (INVs).

verification.description: Verification checklist items aggregated into governance.md ## Verification Checklist. Format: '[ ] {what to check} (ref: ID)'. Extracted from ## Confirmation (ADRs) and ## Enforcement (INVs).

text.description: The directive sentence as it will appear in compiled governance, ≤ 500 characters. Includes its own ref tail (e.g., '(ref: ADR-018)'). The previous 200-char ceiling proved too tight on real-world corpora — natural-language rules with multiple clauses (e.g. INV-003's hook protocol rule) routinely run 200–280 chars. 500 is comfortable headroom; rules that would exceed it should be split.

line_start.description: 1-indexed line number in the parent .md where the directive's source text begins.

line_end.description: 1-indexed line number where the source text ends. Must be ≥ line_start. /edikt:doctor verifies this is within the parent file's line count.

quote.description: Verbatim text from the parent .md between line_start and line_end. Used by /edikt:doctor for drift detection (resync triggers when the live quote no longer matches the recorded quote).

directives.description: Directive list extracted from the parent .md. May be empty (an artifact with no enforceable directives is reported by /edikt:doctor as a warning, not an error). Note: line_end ≥ line_start and line_end ≤ parent_file_line_count are enforced by /edikt:doctor and the compile resync pass — JSON Schema 2020-12 has no standard cross-property comparator.
